message:
test(harness): loader_edges harness-matrix + in-process oracle (bd-k2d8)

Adds two execution paths to loader_edges_conformance_test (previously
had only schema/coverage validation):

  loader_edges_fixture_cases_match_execute_fixture_case
    In-process oracle through `frankenlibc_fixture_exec`.
  loader_edges_fixture_executes_with_host_parity_via_harness_matrix
    Isolated subprocess through `harness conformance-matrix-case`.

Both normalize `expected_output` (JSON) via expected_output_to_string()
since loader_edges fixtures use mixed string/integer outputs.

Carve-outs:
  * case_note_indicates_impl_defined() — dlclose-double-close style
    cases whose note marks the outcome implementation-defined. We
    still pin impl_output against the fixture; host parity is relaxed
    because glibc may legally return a different value.
  * KNOWN_IMPL_GAPS table — bd-oraci tracks a real dladdr gap where
    frankenlibc returns 0 for a valid function pointer while glibc
    (and the fixture) expect nonzero. That specific case is skipped
    here and logged until bd-oraci lands the fix.

Bead bd-oraci filed for the dladdr gap exposed during this work.

Verification (rch + isolated CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_libc_4):
  cargo test  -p frankenlibc-harness --test loader_edges_conformance_test
                                                     13 passed
  cargo clippy -p frankenlibc-harness --all-targets -- -D warnings  clean
  cargo fmt --check -p frankenlibc-harness          clean
  ubs <touched>                                       exit 0

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
